summary: End-to-end testing of new Marketplace feature (AI Characters & Worlds + 4-tier licensing + Creator Earnings). Backend: 63/63 tests passing (27 cinemaforge + 24 new marketplace + 12 tips). Frontend: full marketplace browse → asset detail → license checkout flow verified, including unauth redirect to /auth, authed redirect to checkout.stripe.com, cancelled-toast on ?license_cancelled=1, and Profile /earnings/licenses tabs with $0 stat cards + disabled withdraw button. One small regression fixed in test_cinemaforge.py (seed no longer returns 'skipped' — it now always runs to seed characters/worlds and reports inserted=0 on idempotent calls). No critical bugs found.

minor.issue: No input validation on image_url (a bare string like 'https://x' passes). Consider HttpUrl / regex validation to avoid broken cards on the marketplace grid.
No pagination or filtering by creator / tags / search yet. Hard-coded limit=60. Will not scale past a few hundred assets.
origin_url is trusted (open-redirect via Stripe success/cancel URLs); also carried over from tips. Validate against an allow-list.
STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET still not configured — signature effectively unverified in production (carried over).
Unauthenticated — any party with a session_id can trigger a Stripe retrieve attempt. Same concern as /api/tips/status.

issues: TEST_-prefixed user-generated characters (with image_url='https://x') show broken hero images. Not a real-user bug, just test artefact pollution. Suggest backend validation of image_url to prevent broken cards from non-test users.

action_items: (Optional, code-health) Validate image_url with HttpUrl on POST /api/characters and POST /api/worlds.
(Optional) Add pagination + filter (creator, tags, search) to /api/characters and /api/worlds.
(Carried over) Tighten origin_url validation on /api/licenses/checkout and /api/tips/checkout.
(Carried over) server.py is now ~975 lines — split into routers (auth/movies/ai/tips/marketplace/seed).
critical_code_review_comments: _credit_license is parallel to _credit_tip — clean, idempotent (gated on tx.credited flag), and only increments asset.license_count when payment_status=='paid'. Looks correct.
Webhook handler now correctly branches on tx.kind ('tip' vs 'license') — fallback default of 'tip' is acceptable for legacy rows.
/api/licenses/checkout correctly blocks creators from buying their own asset (400). Good.
/api/licenses/status uses the same try/except db_fallback pattern as tips_status — consistent with prior fix.
server.py has grown to 975 lines — split into multiple routers is now overdue for maintainability.
Frontend AssetDetail.jsx polls /licenses/status up to 6 times after Stripe return — same pattern as MovieDetail tip flow. Toast 'License is processing' shown if not paid by attempt 6 — graceful.
Profile.jsx Earnings tab cleanly renders 3 stat cards + 2 recent-transaction lists with empty-state messaging; Withdraw button correctly disabled when grand_total <= 0.
No race condition between status poll and webhook because _credit_license short-circuits if credited already true.
Earnings endpoint hydrates asset name/image for license rows but not for tip rows — minor inconsistency (tips show only movie_id in the UI list).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_cinemaforge.py (updated test_seed_idempotent to match new always-run-seed behaviour)

success_rate.frontend: 100% on all tested flows (sidebar nav, grid, tabs, asset detail, tier picker, unauth redirect, authed → stripe.com, cancel toast, profile tabs, earnings cards, withdraw disabled)

test_credentials: Tests register fresh users dynamically: test_buyer_<hex>@cinemaforge.io / test_seller_<hex>@cinemaforge.io / Pass1234!. UI test created test_ui_<8chars>@cinemaforge.io.
seed_data_creation: Reused /api/seed (now idempotent for movies AND characters AND worlds). Backend tests created transient seller-owned characters/worlds and one buyer license checkout session (not paid).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All marketplace endpoints verified: GET /api/characters (>=8), GET /api/worlds (>=6), GET/POST detail/create, /api/marketplace/tiers (4+4 with correct amounts 3/8/15/40 and 5/15/25/75), /api/licenses/checkout (auth, 400 on own-asset/bad-tier/bad-type, 404 unknown asset, returns stripe url), /api/licenses/status (200 + db_fallback for fresh session, 404 for unknown, idempotent on 5 calls), /api/licenses/my (auth required, empty for new user), /api/creator/earnings (zero for new user, correct shape). Webhook routing tested only indirectly via test_stripe_webhook_handles_bad_signature in tips (signature rejection); paid-flow not exercised because checkout was never completed. Frontend: marketplace-page, mkt-tab-{characters,worlds}, mkt-card-*, asset-detail-*, tier-picker, tier-{one_time,series,feature,lifetime}, license-confirm, profile-tabs, tab-{forges,earnings,licenses}, earnings-grid, earn-{tips,licenses,total}, withdraw-btn, nav-marketplace — all confirmed.
rca of the issue: Only one pre-existing test broke (test_seed_idempotent) because the iteration_4 seed refactor intentionally drops the `skipped: True` short-circuit so the route can run the character/world seed block even after movies were already inserted. Test updated to assert {ok: True, inserted: 0} instead. No code bug.
